channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Equinox Announces Cancellation of Altered TCG Roots of Corruption Campaign, and The End of The Game
item.content:encoded:
After several years of support, The Altered TCG is coming to an end. Developer Equinox has announced that due to the lack of funding from its latest crowdfunding campaign, the game will not be receiving any new cards.

The news on the future of The Altered TCG is broken up into two parts. First is the the conclusion of the Roots of Corruption campaign on the crowdfunding platform Gamefound. This campaign was meant to produce the sixth official set of cards for the game. The game concluded with  €421,113 raised, more than eight times its initial funding goal of  €50,000.

On paper, this would mean that Altered would have more than enough to keep going. Except, the numbers are complicated than that. On its official website, Equinox's founder, Régis Bonnessée, went into further detail, stating the overall funding was the sum of the crowdfunding goal alongside €680,000 from various professional partners within the industry for a total of €1.1 million.

The problem is, if the Altered TCG were to continue into the future, that sum needed to be at least €2 million. Simply put, the numbers just aren't there.

This announcement marks a bitter end for the entire project. Altered turned heads in 2024 by being the most funded TCG project ever on Kickstarter, raising over $6.7 million. In addition, it aimed to bring a different approach to card collecting with the ability to print more cards and to trade access to printing those cards on a digital storefront.

“I know that the settling silence brings its share of questions: what will happen to your digital collections? To the future on Board Game Arena? To the legacy of this universe? We are not going to leave you in a vacuum. We simply need some time to digest this moment, to properly close this chapter, and to provide you with clear and respectful answers.

Thank you for every card played, for every smile exchanged, and for everything you put of yourselves into this adventure. It was an honor to imagine it with you.”

Despite positive reviews, the Altered TCG had a lot going against it. The TCG market is deeply entrenched in behemoths like Magic: The Gathering and Pokemon. A game based on an original IP would be fighting an uphill battle in such a market. In addition, the digital storefront was heavily delayed, only going live last April, which greatly stymied the collector market.

There is also the uncomfortable truth that a lot of the mass media landscape is driven by recognizable properties. The only recent TCGs to have gained a foothold in such a market has been the licensed One Piece card game, based on the globally acclaimed manga by Eiichiro Oda, and Disney Lorcana, a game packed to the brim with Walt Disney nostalgia.

Punctuating this further is the newest record holder for the most crowdfunded TCG on Kickstarter: Weird Co.'s Cyberpunk Trading Card Game at over $10 million and growing.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/equinox-announces-cancellation-of-altered-tcg-roots-of-corruption-campaign-and-end-of
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
